explanation: VNS Health — Enterprise Applications Analyst (Workday & Enterprise Systems) is marked `network_or_research_first` because it combines a technical match score of 76 with a personal strategy score of 74, producing a combined score of 75. The role code `support-workday` maps to Paul's current positioning around BA/BSA, application support, production readiness, regulated enterprise environments, and practical AI-assisted workflow interest where relevant.
why_attractive: Application/production support role family aligns with FRBNY runbook, incident, release, and support background.
Employer type is more likely to offer structured benefits; verify directly before applying.
risk_flags: Specialized platform/domain appears; tailor carefully and avoid overstating direct hands-on experience.
Workday-specific role may require ramp-up framing from enterprise application support rather than direct Workday administration.
resume_focus: FRBNY application support and production support experience.
Release validation, deployment readiness, runbooks, and post-release health checks.
Incident coordination across DevOps, QA, database, infrastructure, operations, and business stakeholders.
Linux, Oracle, AWS-connected services, ServiceNow, and monitoring/readiness language where truthful.
Enterprise application support and workflow analysis as a bridge to Workday; avoid claiming direct Workday administration.
interview_story_focus: FRBNY production support story: issue detection, log/data validation, escalation, communication, and closure.
Release readiness story: runbook, deployment validation, post-release health checks, rollback/DR awareness.
application_priority: Research/network first before investing in full tailoring.
next_action: Research employer/role before spending time on full tailoring.
